coredata.dc:title: Colorimetric and smartphone-visual detection of biothiols in human serum and red wine based on POD-like activity of Fe-CDs
coredata.prism:publicationName: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy
coredata.prism:copyright: © 2024 Elsevier B.V. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.
coredata.prism:publisher: Elsevier B.V. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.
coredata.dc:creator: Xie, Sijia
Lei, Qiyu
Zeng, Linyu
Xiong, Hai
coredata.dc:description: An efficient mimic peroxidase platform of Fe-CDs/GOx based on the hybrid cascade system to produce in-situ H2O2 for the visualized detection of glucose has been also developed in our group. Herein, the [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB] system was further performed to detect the biothiols (GSH, Cys, and Hcy) as representatives of the –SH group. The result exhibits that the higher concentration of biothiols can cause the absorbance signal at 652 nm to decrease, and the deep-blue color of oxTMB turns green until it is faded in 20 min by the naked eye. The colorimetric method showed the LOD are 0.54, 0.29, and 1.41 μM for GSH, Cys, and Hcy respectively, without interference in the presence of other amino acids. The smartphone-based and paper-based determination display good respect for biothiols in human serum or GSH in red wine. These simple and convenient strategies provide potential applications in the fields of clinical diagnosis and foods.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Fe-CDs
Biothiols
Colorimetric sensing
Mobile platform
Visual detection

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Colorimetric sensing for the determination of GSH, Cys, and Hcy
Smartphone-based determination of GSH/Cys/Hcy in human serum
Paper-based determination of GSH/Cys/Hcy in human serum or red wine
Colorimetric sensing for the determination of GSH, Cys, and Hcy
Smartphone-based determination of GSH/Cys/Hcy in human serum
Paper-based determination of GSH in wine

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: This work is supported by the Science and Technology Innovation Commission of Shenzhen, China (JCYJ20210324095607021 and 20231121191245001 to HX), and the Special Project of Key Fields of Universities in Guangdong Province, China (2021ZDZX2047 to HX). The use of the human serum samples used in this study was approved according to the guidelines of the China Ethical Committee by the Regional Ethic Board at Shenzhen University (ethical permissions PN-202400004). The participant provided written informed consent to participate in the detection of biothiols.

This work is supported by the Science and Technology Innovation Commission of Shenzhen, China ( JCYJ20210324095607021 and 20231121191245001 to HX), and the Special Project of Key Fields of Universities in Guangdong Province, China ( 2021ZDZX2047 to HX).

ce:caption.ce:simple-para:
(a) The UV–vis absorption at 652 nm of Fe-CDs with different ROS scavengers (TBA, PBQ, and NaN3, 5 mM). Reaction condition: 0.1 mg/mL of Fe-CDs, 0.25 mM TMB, 0.1 mM H2O2, pH = 3.5. (b) EPR spectra of the Fe-CDs induced
O2 − generation with H2O2.

UV–Vis absorption spectra of the [Fe-CDs + TMB] reaction system with the different concentrations of (a) GSH, (b) Cys, and (c) Hcy. The UV–Vis absorption at 652 nm changed with time (0–30 min) with the different concentrations of (d) GSH, (e) Cys, and (f) Hcy.
The corresponding graphic images of GSH, Cys, and Hcy treated in a mixture system of [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB].

The value of parameters (R, G, and B) vs different concentrations of (a) GSH, (b) Cys, and (c) Hcy (0–60 µM). Calibration plot between analytical signal (RS–RB) and different concentrations of (d) GSH, (e) Cys and (f) Hcy (1–60 µM).
The sensitivity of colorimetric papers for GSH, Cys, and Hcy detection based on the reaction system of [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB].
(a) Selectivity of the constructed colorimetric-based detection platform. Anti-interference ability of (b) GSH, (c) Cys, and (d) Hcy.
Color change in added GSH and red wine due to their different GSH concentration.
Schematic graph for the GSH detection mechanism based on the mixture system of [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB].
Detection methods, sensors, and LOD for biothiols detection by comparing Fe-CDs and other Metal-mediated CDs.
Comparison of the traditional methods for GSH detection in wines.

head.ce:title: Colorimetric and smartphone-visual detection of biothiols in human serum and red wine based on POD-like activity of Fe-CDs

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Institute for Advanced Study, Shenzhen University, Shenzhen 518060, PR China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Institute for Advanced Study, Shenzhen University, Shenzhen 518060, PR China

ce:footnote.ce:note-para: S.X. and Q.L. contributed equally to this work.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: An efficient mimic peroxidase platform of Fe-CDs/GOx based on the hybrid cascade system to produce in-situ H2O2 for the visualized detection of glucose has been also developed in our group. Herein, the [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB] system was further performed to detect the biothiols (GSH, Cys, and Hcy) as representatives of the –SH group. The result exhibits that the higher concentration of biothiols can cause the absorbance signal at 652 nm to decrease, and the deep-blue color of oxTMB turns green until it is faded in 20 min by the naked eye. The colorimetric method showed the LOD are 0.54, 0.29, and 1.41 μM for GSH, Cys, and Hcy respectively, without interference in the presence of other amino acids. The smartphone-based and paper-based determination display good respect for biothiols in human serum or GSH in red wine. These simple and convenient strategies provide potential applications in the fields of clinical diagnosis and foods.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: No data was used for the research described in the article.

ce:section.ce:para: Biothiol are thiol-containing amino acid or oligopeptide, are also important components of many proteins and bio-active small molecules that maintain redox homeostasis in biological systems [1]. Alterations in intracellular biothiol levels may be associated with many underlying diseases. Glutathione (GSH), Cysteine (Cys), and Hcyteine (Hcy) are treated as the most typical representatives. Especially, the abnormal concentration of GSH is closely associated with various diseases including cancer, acquired immune deficiency syndrome (AIDS), cardiovascular problems, liver damage, aging, diabetes, etc [2–4]. It also serves as a significant cancer-related biomarker that plays a pivotal role in preserving intracellular homeostasis. Furthermore, the GSH system is one of the most powerful intracellular antioxidant systems, and GSH has been discovered to eliminate the reactive of reactive oxygen species (ROS). Monitoring intracellular GSH levels in real-time is crucial due to the potential negative impacts on food safety and human health [5–7]. Therefore, the detection of biothiols is closely related to human physiological health, and it is of great significance to develop simple, sensitive, and selective detection methods.

Various analytical methodologies have been used for the selective detection of biothiols, such as Electrochemistry [8], Liquid chromatography [9], Mass spectroscopy [10], Raman spectroscopy [11], chemiluminescence [12], Fluorescence (FL) spectroscopy [13,14], colorimetry [15,16], etc. Recently, CDs have attracted widespread attention due to their merits of facile preparation and easy surface modification, remarkable optical characteristics, chemical inertness, as well as negligible cytotoxicity and good biocompatibility [17–21]. Metal-doped CDs (M–CDs) facilely integrate the advantages of metal ions and CDs, and have been treated as versatile platforms to apply in the rapid response of metal ions and antibiotics, visual detection of glucose, antibacterial testing, fluorescent sensors, and bioimaging, etc [22–25].

Up till now, several researchers have been published related to the detection of GSH metal-mediated CDs through an “on-off-on” mechanism [26–30]. For example, our previous work has reported a novel fluorescent probe utilizing silver-mediated red emissive carbon dots (RCDs@Ag+) for the discrimination of cancer cells from normal cells through the bioimaging of GSH [30]. The colorimetric method can determine the initial results even by the naked eye, due to its simple, practical, and low cost, many research or review articles have been published to highlight the successful applications of nano-particles or nanozyme-based colorimetric techniques, such as the detection of environmental pollutants including heavy metal ions, anions, microbial contaminations, pathogen, residual pesticide or antibiotic in a food matrix, biomolecules (amino acids, vitamins, and proteins) [24,31–36].

Furthermore, the recently emerging colorimetric sensor improves the detection and recognition of biothiols, and the mechanism of biothiol detection is chromogenic signal change based on the interaction between biothiol and metal ions [37,38]. Our previous work has also reported that the Fe-Codoped carbon dots (Fe-CDs) exhibited enhanced peroxidase-like activity owing to the synergistic effect between Fe2+ and CDs by colorimetry device [24]. Due to the strong binding affinity between biothiols and Fe2+ as well as decreasing the formation of
O2 − species, in this study, the catalytic activities of Fe-CDs could be inhibited by different biothiols to diverse degrees. In the presence of H2O2 and TMB, the Fe-CDs exhibited significant adhesion to selectively detect the biothiols (GSH, Cys, Hcy) by three detection approaches including colorimetric determination, smartphone-based determination, and paper-based determination, enabling its application in the fields of clinical diagnosis and foods.

Glutathione (GSH), Cysteine (Cys), Homocysteine (Hcy), Glycine (Gly), Lycine (Ly), Methionine (Me), Proline (Pro), Serine (Se), Threonine (Th), Tyrosine (Ty), Tert butyl alcohol (TBA), 1, 4-Benzoquinone (PBQ), and activated-carbon were purchased from Shanghai Macklin Biochemical Co., Ltd. Glycine was purchased from Shanghai Hushi Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. Proline and Sodium azide were purchased from Aladdin Reagent Co., Ltd. Threonine was from Shanghai Hanhong Scientific Co., Ltd. Wine was purchased from Penfolds (Treasury Wine Eatates).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Colorimetric sensing for the determination of GSH, Cys, and Hcy
ce:section.ce:para: To detect and distinguish GSH/Cys/Hcy, the following experimental procedure was employed. As the prepared GSH at various concentrations (0, 0.5, 1, 5, 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60 μM), the Fe-CDs in an acetate buffer (pH = 3.5, 0.1 M) were added to each of them, then H2O2 was participated in. Further, TMB was added to the above solution, and the final concentration of Fe-CDs, H2O2, and TMB was 0.1 mg/mL, 0.1 mM, and 0.5 mM respectively. Stirring at room temperature for 30 min, the mixed solution was measured by UV–vis absorption (300–800 nm) to monitor the variation of catalytic activity, and the kinetics study (from 0 to 30 min) was also performed. The detection assay of Cys and Hcy were the same as GSH.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Smartphone-based determination of GSH/Cys/Hcy in human serum
ce:section.ce:para: To operate the smartphone-based determination of GSH, the following experimental procedure was performed. As preparing different concentrations of GSH (0, 0.5, 1, 5, 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60 μM), each 100 μL of human serum in a cuvette was diluted to 100-fold to decrease intrinsic GSH level. And then, 100 μL Fe-CDs, 1.1 mL acetate buffer (pH = 3.5, 0.1 M), H2O2 (0.1 mM), and 100 μL of TMB were added to the above cuvettes with each 1.5 mL of the total volume, the final concentration of Fe-CDs, H2O2, and TMB was 0.1 mg/mL, 0.1 mM and 0.5 mM respectively. Stirring at room temperature for 30 min, each cuvette was put into a camera obscura UV analyzer which provides a light illumination for image capture. The images were captured by a smartphone facilitated with a 64-megapixel camera with Android 13 and UI 4.0 operating system. After that, the local RGB software was used to analyze the RGB (R: Red; G: Green and B: Blue) color intensity. The operations assay of Cys and Hcy were the same as GSH.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Paper-based determination of GSH/Cys/Hcy in human serum or red wine
ce:section.ce:para: For the paper-based determination of GSH, Cys, and Hcy in human serum or red wine, firstly, various ordinary laboratory filter papers were cut into wafers with 6 mm diameter by a hole puncher. Secondly, 200 μL of Fe-CDs (final concentration: 0.1 mg/ml), 1.4 mL of acetate buffer (pH = 3.5, 0.1 M), 200 μL of H2O2 (final concentration: 0.1 mM) and 200 μL TMB (final concentration: 0.5 mM) were prepared. Thirdly, immersing the wafers into the above solution mixed with human serum or red wine at room temperature for 2 h, the wafers were changed to blue and taken out to remove redundant water, and then placed in a plastic paper. Lastly, the GSH, Cys, and Hcy with different concentrations (0, 10, 50, 80, 100, 200, 300, 400 μM) were dropped on the wafers respectively. After 1.5 h, removing superfluous water on the wafers, they were placed on white paper to observe the color change with the naked eye.

To explore the catalytic mechanism of POD-like activity of Fe-CDs, the three oxidative species of hydroxyl radicals (
OH), superoxide anion radicals (
O2 −), and singlet oxygen (1O2) were evaluated to identify which one contributed to the oxidation reaction. Reactive oxygen species (ROS) scavengers of Tert butyl alcohol (TBA), 1, 4-Benzoquinone (PBQ), and Sodium azide (NaN3) were used as a remover for
OH,
O2 −, and 1O2, respectively [39,40]. As shown in Fig. 1 a, the addition of TBA and NaN3 showed the UV–vis absorption almost the same as that of the control sample. At line 3, the absorbance intensity was obviously decreased in the case of PBQ, which revealed the process of generated
O2 − in the catalytic oxidation of H2O2 by the Fe-CDs. To authoritatively prove the result, the EPR spectroscopy was performed to confirm the production of
O2 − species. As illustrated in Fig. 1b, when Fe-CDs, H2O2, and trapping agents (DMPO) coexisted, the
O2 − signal appeared as a red line. While there is no obvious signal as a black line. The results demonstrated the Fe-CDs exhibit great POD-like activity by generating
O2 − in the presence of H2O2. In the case of GSH and [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB], GSH could be oxidized into GSSG based on the consumption of H2O2, decreasing the formation of
O2 − species, thus TMB can’t be oxidized to oxTMB (blue). Due to the strong binding affinity between biothiols and Fe2+ as well as the decreased concentration of
O2 −, the catalytic activities of Fe-CDs could be inhibited by different biothiols to diverse degrees.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Colorimetric sensing for the determination of GSH, Cys, and Hcy
ce:section.ce:para: Inspired by the excellent POD-like activity of Fe-CDs, biothiols (GSH, Cys, and Hcy) as representatives of the –SH group were performed to study its analytical performances. As presented in Fig. 2a–c , with the increasing concentration of GSH/Cys/Hcy in the [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB] reaction system, the absorbance signal at 652 nm gradually decreased, and the deep blue color was fading by the naked eyes. Further, the time-course assay showed the absorbance signal gradually enhanced with the increasing reaction time until it was quickly saturated in 20 min. These results indicated higher concentration of GSH/Cys/Hcy would thoroughly restrain the colorimetric reaction of TMB (Fig. 2d–f).

Fig. S1a–c displayed the change of absorbance intensity at 652 nm, upon the addition of different concentrations of GSH/Cys/Hcy respectively. Here came three linear equations: (1) Abs = −0.01206 [GSH] + 1.87038 (R2 = 0.995); (2) Abs = −0.01282 [Cys] + 1.67567 (R2 = 0.996); (3) Abs = −000957 [Hcy] + 1.80613 (R2 = 0.976) in the range of 1–60 µM each other, and the detection of limit (LOD) were 0.54, 0.29, 1.41 μM for GSH, Cys and Hcy respectively. (The LOD was calculated as the lowest analyte quantity that can be distinguished from the blank using the formula LOD = 3ɛ/k, where ɛrepresents the standard deviation of the response value, and k represents the linear slope). As shown in Table 1 , compared to other reported Metal-mediated CDs, Fe-CDs performed a good LOD and a simple strategy for biothiols detection.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Smartphone-based determination of GSH/Cys/Hcy in human serum
ce:section.ce:para: Recently, more smartphone-based detection tools have been developed and extensively applied, suffering from the inspiration of the splendid POD-like activity of Fe-CDs, we herein utilized the GSH/Cys/Hcy as targets to study the analytical performances on the mixture system of [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB]. Various levels of GSH were added to the above system. As shown in Scheme 1 , the sensing test of GSH inhibition was based on the oxidation reaction of TMB which is catalyzed by the [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB] system. According to the color change with blue fading, a smartphone was used to take photos, and the local RGB software can analyze the color signals. Further, the operations for smartphone-based determination of Cys and Hcy were the same as GSH.

To obtain accurate visualization by the naked eye, a smartphone-based determination of GSH/Cys/Hcy in human serum was performed and the graphic images were displayed in Fig. 3 . With the increasing concentration of GSH/Cys/Hcy (0–60 µM), the blue color was gradually fading. We utilized RGB software to analyze the color signals. The R, G, and B color intensities are shown in Fig. 4a–c , the R color is a complementary color for the blue color of the reaction liquid, thus the R intensity showed an obvious response than the G and B colors made. To make the results clearer, the intensity distance of red colors (RS–RB) between the tested samples (RS) and blank (RB) can be analyzed by the record signals. As observed in Fig. 4d–f, the calibration plot between the analytical signal of RS–RB and GSH/Cys/Hcy concentration exhibited good linearity, as following the equations: (1) RS–RB = 1.8766 [GSH]–0.25116 (R2 = 0.996), (2) RS–RB = 1.32632 [Cys]–2.85354 (R2 = 0.985), (3) RS–RB = 0.41789 [Hcy]–1.22847(R2 = 0.993) for GSH, Cys and Hcy respectively. And then, certain GSH/Cys/Hcy (0–60 µM) were added into human serum samples, the results showed that the satisfactory recovery rate was from 90.00 % to 98.74 % for GSH, 90.70 % to 98.62 % for Cys, and 98.67 % to 99.87 % for Hcy. Further, the relative standard deviation (RSD) was less than 3.3 % (Table S1). Therefore, the smartphone-based determination displayed good sensing for human serum-grade GSH, Cys, and Hcy, indicating its potential feasibility in real chemical and biologicalanalytes.

To affirm the potential ability of papers for GSH, Cys, and Hcy based on the reaction system of [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB], we generated paper-based analytical devices (PADs) to detect GSH, Cys, and Hcy. As illustrated in Fig. 5 , each of GSH/Cys/Hcy (0–400 µM) was added into the above system to keep stirring, then dropped on the papers to be existing on the light-blue color due to the restrained ox-TMB. In the absence of GSH/Cys/Hcy, the color on the paper was dark blue, while the color became fading with the increasing concentration of biothiols. Furthermore, the added concentration was going up to a limit, and the color on the papers disappeared completely. Therefore, the level of GSH, Cys, and Hcy can be monitored by responding to the color change of the colorimetric papers. In general, paper-based colorimetric is also a good sensing platform to provide promising and effective methods for the trace detection of GSH, Cys, and Hcy.

A series of natural amino acids including Gly, Ly, Me, Pro, Se, Th, and Ty were treated as interfering substances to evaluate the selectivity and anti-interference ability of the detection platform of [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB] system. Each of the biothiols or amino acids was performed to detect by UV–vis absorption (300–800 nm), according to the standard operation in section 3.2. The absorption alterations of the selected solution in the highest absorbance at 625 nm were recorded as depicted in Fig. 6 a (▴Abs = Absorbace(Control)–Absorbace(Samples)). The obtained results revealed that the addition of biothiols prompted significant variations in the UV–visible spectrum of the [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB] system, accompanied by a change in color visible to the naked eye. Interestingly, the addition of biothiols led to the appearance of the highest absorption band, conversely, the addition of other tested amino acids did not produce any significant changes and the solution's color remained unaffected.

As a comparison, the mixed system with the Fe-CDs in an acetate buffer (pH = 3.5, 0.1 M), and 0.1 mM of H2O2 was added to each of the biothiols or amino acids (40 µM) mixed the 100-fold dilution of human serum, and TMB (0.5 mM) was participated in. Remarkably, this observation reveals minimal changes in the absorbance of [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB] system treated with various amino acids along with GSH, compared to those treated with GSH alone, as shown in Fig. 6b. The same results can be found in the case of Cys, and Hcy (Fig. 6c and 6d). These findings suggest exceptional selectivity of synthesized Fe-CDs towards biothiols even in the presence of similar amino acids that do not interfere in the detection of biothiols.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Paper-based determination of GSH in wine

ce:section.ce:para:
Except for colorimetric detection of H2O2 and glucose, Fe-CDs are further utilized to selectively detect the biothiols (GSH, Cys, Hcy) in human serum or red wine by smartphone-based determination, and paper-based determination. The EPR spectroscopy is performed to confirm the production of
O2 − species, exploring the catalytic mechanism of POD-like activity of Fe-CDs in the presence of H2O2. For the [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB] reaction system, four conclusions can be summarized, such as 1). The high concentration of biothiols causes the highest absorbance signal at 652 nm to decrease and restrain the colorimetric reaction of TMB to bring about the deep-blue color to be faded in 20 min. The result showed the LOD is 0.54, 0.29, and 1.41 μM for GSH, Cys, and Hcy respectively, which is more sensitive than most of the current reported sensors; 2). For the selective detection of biothiols, the [Fe-CDs + H2O2 + TMB] system does not exhibit any interference in the presence of similar amino acids; 3). The smartphone-based determination displays good sensing for human serum-grade GSH, Cys, and Hcy; 4). Paper-based analytical devices provide a possible respect for simple and convenient GSH detection in red wine. The impact of food disinfection on the beneficial biothiol contents consumed daily will be worth investigating, including the design of optimum detection methods to simultaneously achieve sufficient bacterial disinfection while minimizing the loss of beneficial biothiols.

Sijia Xie: Investigation, methodology, experiments, data analysis, and writing original draft. Qiyu Lei: Experiments, visualization, data analysis. Linyu Zeng: Data analysis. Hai Xiong: Conceptualization, funding acquisition, supervision, writing original draft, and writing – review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work is supported by the Science and Technology Innovation Commission of Shenzhen, China (JCYJ20210324095607021 and 20231121191245001 to HX), and the Special Project of Key Fields of Universities in Guangdong Province, China (2021ZDZX2047 to HX).

ce:section.ce:para: The use of the human serum samples used in this study was approved according to the guidelines of the China Ethical Committee by the Regional Ethic Board at Shenzhen University (ethical permissions PN-202400004). The participant provided written informed consent to participate in the detection of biothiols.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.saa.2024.125280.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
Supplementary Data 1

Org. Biomol. Chem.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Glutathione as a marker for human disease
Adv. Clin. Chem.

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Teskey, G.; Abrahem, R.; Cao, R.; Gyurjian, K.; Islamoglu, H.; Lucero, M.; Martinez, A.; Paredes, E.; Salaiz, O.; Robinson, B. Glutathione as a marker for human disease. Adv. Clin. Chem. 2018, 87, 141-159.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: The importance of glutathione in human disease
Biomed. Pharmacother.

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Townsend, D. M.; Tew, K. D.; Tapiero, H. The importance of glutathione in human disease. Biomed. Pharmacother. 2003, 57 (3-4), 145-155.

Anal. Chem.

Anal. Chem.

Chem. Soc. Rev.

ACS Sens. 2022, 7 (8), 2465-2474.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: An accurate mass spectrometric approach for the simultaneous comparison of GSH, Cys, and Hcy in LO2 cells and HepG2 cells using new NPSP isotope probes
